questions: You mentioned being skeptical about content-addressed hashing at first—what specifically made the idempotency argument win you over versus just deduplicating with a simple counter?
The bare→boosted score spreads were massive (+14 to +25 points). Did those gaps surprise you, or did you expect that kind of delta based on how the system was designed?

You said the Router v2 fix was your 'first data-driven prompt change'—what would have happened to your iteration speed if you'd only run the golden set once instead of catching that intermittent 5/6 pattern?
